auth.name: Name

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.manageDescription: Manage users, API keys, and audit logs
dashboard.titleAndFileNameRequired: Title and file name are required

landing.title: PlainVault
landing.subtitle: Store secrets, configs, and secure team notes — without the complexity.
landing.heroDescription: Store API keys, credentials, and configurations in one secure place. Role-based access, full version history, and complete audit trails — without the complexity.
landing.featuresTitle: Everything you need, nothing you don't
landing.featuresDescription: PlainVault strips away the complexity of secret management while keeping enterprise-grade security.
landing.featureRbacDescription: Granular permissions with ADMIN, DEVELOPER, and VIEWER roles. Control who can view, edit, or manage your secrets.
landing.featureHistoryDescription: Every change is tracked. Compare revisions, review change summaries, and restore previous versions instantly.
landing.featureCategoriesDescription: Group secrets by environment, service, or team. Color-coded categories keep your vault organized at scale.
landing.featureAuditDescription: Complete visibility into who accessed or modified what. Every action is logged for compliance and security reviews.
landing.howItWorksDescription: Get started in minutes, not hours.
landing.stepAccountDescription: Register with your email. An admin approves new users and assigns their role.
landing.stepOrganizeDescription: Create categories for different environments, services, or teams.
landing.stepManageDescription: Store API keys, credentials, and configs. Edit with automatic version tracking.
landing.stepAuditDescription: Review the full history of changes and maintain compliance with complete audit logs.
landing.ctaDescription: Join teams who trust PlainVault to manage their most sensitive data.
landing.footerDescription: Secure vault management for teams. Built with care.

step1.beforeLink: Start by requesting access — go to

security.title: Security model
security.encryptionAtRest: All file content is encrypted at rest with AES-256-GCM before it is stored.

viewerMasking.afterRole: your view is masked to reduce accidental leaks.

viewerShownAs.afterRole: will see sensitive values hidden like this:

security.devAdminSeeRaw: can view raw values when needed.

docs.guideIntro: A clean internal vault for storing secrets, configs, and secure team notes. All content is encrypted with AES-256-GCM and access is controlled by role-based permissions.

requesting.title: Requesting an account

step3.afterButton: — your account will be marked as

requesting.step4: Wait for an admin to approve your registration.

pendingStatus.title: Pending approval
pendingStatus.description: While your account is pending, you can't sign in yet. Once approved, you'll be able to log in normally.

cards.admin: Admins manage access and policies. They can approve users, manage categories, issue raw-file API keys, and view audit logs.
cards.developer: Developers can create and edit files, view actual values, manage their own API keys, and browse revision history.
cards.viewer: Viewers can browse files safely with masking and create read-only API keys, but can't create or edit files.

create.title: Create a file
create.step3: Enter a title and file name.
create.step4: Choose a content type and category.

view.title: View content (masked vs raw)
view.item1: Files are listed on the dashboard and grouped by category.

item4.afterButton: to toggle between masked and raw views (if permitted).

description.afterRole: you can still read files safely — sensitive values in
description.afterCode: style formats are masked in the UI.

categoriesSection.title: Organize with categories

description.afterRole: and help your team group files by environment, service, or purpose.

categoriesSection.note: Tip: Use colors to distinguish environments at a glance.

historySection.title: History & revisions
historySection.description: Every edit creates a new revision. You can compare changes and restore older content at any time.

steps.step1: Open a file you want to review.
steps.step3: Select a revision to view its diff.

textSearch.title: Text search
textSearch.description: Find files by title, file name, or content.

categoryFilter.title: Category filter
categoryFilter.description: Narrow results to a specific category.

combined.title: Combine filters
combined.description: Use text search and category filtering together for faster navigation.

apiKeysSection.title: API Keys
apiKeysSection.description: Approved users can create and manage their own API keys for CI/CD and other programmatic access. Admins can additionally issue raw-file keys.

steps.step3: Name the key and set an optional expiration.
steps.step4: Copy the key now — you won't be able to see it again.

footer.beforeCode: Send your API key as a bearer token in the Authorization header, for example:

docs.apiSubtitle: PlainVault REST API — session-based authentication via cookies

api.sessionAuthDescription: Most API endpoints use session-based authentication via cookies. After logging in via <loginEndpoint>POST /api/auth/login</loginEndpoint>, include the session cookie in subsequent requests using <curlFlag>-b cookies.txt</curlFlag> (curl), <fetchCredentials>credentials: 'include'</fetchCredentials> (fetch), or <pythonSession>requests.Session()</pythonSession> (Python).
api.apiKeyAuthDescription: For programmatic access (CI/CD pipelines, scripts), use API keys with Bearer token authentication. API keys are created by admins via the UI or the <createKeyEndpoint>POST /api/admin/api-keys</createKeyEndpoint> endpoint.
api.apiKeyScopes: Keys can be scoped to specific operations (currently: <scope>files:read_raw</scope>)
api.apiKeyExpiry: Keys can be set to expire after a configurable number of days
api.apiKeyRevoked: Revoked or expired keys return <status>401 Unauthorized</status>
api.getMetadataUseCase: Get metadata + masked/full content in dashboard
api.includeApiKeyHeader: # Include the API key in the Authorization header
